title: Arrays, Slices, Backing Storage, Append, Copy, and Capacity · Spiral Systems Review
summary: A batch processor must window records, grow buffers, preserve caller data, and avoid retention or aliasing defects. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for an alias-safe batch and buffer toolkit after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use arrays as fixed-length value types and explain how length participates in type identity and copying.
Reason about slice length, capacity, backing storage, nil slices, empty non-nil slices, and observable API differences.
Use two-index and full slice expressions while proving bounds, capacity, and append authority.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Arrays Value Semantics · changed case · Review Arrays Value Semantics
steps.instruction:
review case — A batch slice exposes 4 of 8 records from shared storage. Append one result, mutate one element, clone one boundary, and predict length, capacity, aliasing, and retained storage after every operation.
